tasks.description: Initialize baseline portfolio context and validate constraints. Establish the starting point (Iteration 0) using the CIO’s initial portfolio and confirm it meets structural rules before analysis.

subtasks.description: Retrieve the CIO’s initial portfolio dictionary to establish the baseline (Iteration 0). Ensure you capture all 34 tickers with their conviction levels and long/short flags.

subtasks.description: Validate the portfolio_dict format against the Dictionary Format Rules: double-quoted keys/strings, numeric convictions unquoted, single-line structure, and no trailing commas. Fix formatting if needed.

Compute baseline exposure metrics: total long sum, total short absolute sum, net exposure (longs − shorts), and counts of longs and shorts. Verify counts are within 15–20 longs and 10–15 shorts (informational at this stage) and net exposure proximity to +30%.
Record the baseline as “Portfolio Iteration 0 (Initial)” along with exposure metrics for comparison in later iterations.

tasks.description: Run the full quantitative risk suite on the initial portfolio to establish baseline risk, concentration, diversification, and resilience metrics.

subtasks.description: Run vol_es with default parameters (horizon_days=1, conf=0.99, method="param") to obtain portfolio volatility, VaR, and Expected Shortfall (ES). Record values as baseline.
Run risk_contribution with metric="vol" to decompose total volatility risk and identify top Component Total Risk (CTR%) positions that dominate portfolio risk.
Run risk_contribution with metric="var" to assess tail-risk contributions and confirm whether the same names dominate VaR-based risk.
Run drawdown_profile to extract maximum drawdown, average drawdown, Ulcer Index, and recovery times over the last 2 years. Note any extended recovery episodes.
Calculate the correlation matrix to identify diversification issues and clusters (flag any pairwise correlations > 0.70 and sector/subsector clusters).
Calculate the covariance matrix to support optimization and precise risk budgeting (use for volatility-weighted sizing).
Run stress_test to evaluate performance under market crash scenarios, sector rotations, rate shocks, inflation spikes, and correlation breakdown. Record worst-case impacts and scenario sensitivities.
Synthesize a baseline Vulnerability Register: list high-risk contributors, correlation clusters, unstable drawdown behavior, and adverse stress sensitivities to address in iterations.

tasks.description: Conduct market context research to augment historical analytics with forward-looking insights and catalyze risk-aware hypotheses for rebalancing.

subtasks.description: Research current macro conditions relevant to Consumer Staples (inflation trajectory, consumer confidence, rates, FX) and summarize directional risk implications for staples demand and pricing power.

Investigate subsector trends (beverages, packaged foods, HPC, retail staples, tobacco) to identify relative winners/losers and likely rotation patterns that may affect correlation clusters and stress outcomes.
Search for recent news and earnings insights on the top risk-contributing tickers from Task 2 (both long and short) to validate or challenge exposures.
Synthesize a forward risk outlook linking macro/subsector signals to the baseline vulnerability register (e.g., hedge needs, exposure tilts, idiosyncratic risks).

tasks.description: Design Iteration 1 rebalancing hypotheses to reduce risk concentrations and improve diversification while targeting ~+30% net exposure and maintaining 15–20 longs and 10–15 shorts.

subtasks.description: From Task 2 findings, identify positions for potential decreases/drops (e.g., highest CTR%, overlapping correlations >0.70, adverse stress sensitivity) and articulate the risk rationale.
Identify positions to increase due to diversification benefits (low correlation pairs <0.30), stable fundamentals, and controlled volatility; propose initial increase amounts.
Translate the hypotheses into specific allocation changes (increase/decrease/drop) with precise decimal amounts (e.g., 0.02) ensuring feasibility and alignment with conviction levels.
Construct the Iteration 1 portfolio_dict incorporating all changes. Verify structural rules: 15–20 longs, 10–15 shorts, and net exposure ~+30% using sizing math.
Document the Iteration 1 actionable suggestions list (mapping from initial portfolio), including the exact change amounts and analytical reasons.

tasks.description: Execute the complete risk suite on Iteration 1 and compare to the initial baseline. Decide whether further iteration is required.

subtasks.description: Run vol_es (defaults) on Iteration 1 and record volatility, VaR, and ES. Compare to Iteration 0.
Run risk_contribution with metric="vol" on Iteration 1 to reassess CTR% distribution and confirm reduced concentration.
Run risk_contribution with metric="var" on Iteration 1 to evaluate improvements in tail-risk concentration.
Run drawdown_profile on Iteration 1 to validate improvement in historical downside characteristics.
Recalculate correlation matrix to ensure correlation clusters have moderated and new low-correlation pairs emerged.
Recalculate covariance matrix to support updated risk budgeting and confirm variance reductions.
Run stress_test to verify improved resilience under crash, rotation, rate shock, inflation spike, and correlation breakdown scenarios.
Quantitatively compare Iteration 1 vs Initial across VaR, ES, volatility, CTR%, max drawdown, Ulcer Index, cluster correlations, and stress outcomes; summarize gains and residual issues.
Decision gate: If improvements are insufficient (e.g., top CTR% still high, drawdowns unacceptable, or net exposure off target), proceed to Iteration 2 design.

tasks.description: Design and execute Iteration 2 focused on eliminating residual concentration risks and improving downside protection without sacrificing alpha potential.

subtasks.description: Refine increases/decreases/drops based on Iteration 1 analytics (e.g., further trim high-volatility names, add diversifying shorts/longs, rebalance subsector tilts).
Construct the Iteration 2 portfolio_dict with precise allocation changes and validate structural rules (counts and ~+30% net exposure).
Run the full risk suite (vol_es, risk_contribution vol/var, drawdown_profile, correlation, covariance, stress_test) on Iteration 2 and quantify improvements vs Iterations 0 and 1.
Decision gate: If risk targets not yet met (e.g., VaR/ES still elevated, correlation clusters persist), plan an Iteration 3; otherwise proceed to finalization.

tasks.description: Optional Iteration 3 (if needed) for final tuning to meet low-beta and concentration thresholds while respecting exposure and count rules.

subtasks.description: Implement targeted micro-adjustments (e.g., ±0.005 to ±0.015) to smooth CTR% distribution, reduce pairwise correlations >0.70, and keep net exposure near +0.30.
Rebuild the Iteration 3 portfolio_dict and validate counts (15–20 longs; 10–15 shorts) and exposure math.
Run full risk suite again and ensure progressive improvement across all key metrics vs prior iterations.

tasks.description: Select the final portfolio and perform comprehensive validation with complete documentation of changes and risk improvements.

subtasks.description: Choose the best iteration as the Final Portfolio based on balanced improvements in VaR/ES, drawdowns, correlation clusters, and stress performance while maintaining alpha thesis.
Confirm Final Portfolio structural rules: 15–20 longs, 10–15 shorts, and ~+30% net exposure. Recheck position weights sum and exposure math.
Run the entire risk suite one last time on the Final Portfolio (vol_es, risk_contribution vol/var, drawdown_profile, correlation, covariance, stress_test) to validate results.

Compile a comparative summary Initial vs Final (and intermediate iterations): VaR/ES, total volatility, max drawdown, Ulcer Index, top CTR% names, correlation clusters, and worst-case stress outcomes.

Create the exhaustive actionable suggestions list detailing every allocation increase/decrease (with amounts) and dropped positions with clear analytical reasons (correlation risk, volatility management, diversification benefit).
Perform quality checks: ensure weights/exposures are internally consistent, changes align with risk rationale, and documentation is complete.

tasks.description: Execution tracking, evidence collection, and plan management to ensure auditability and orderly progression through tasks.

subtasks.description: Use evidence tools to capture outputs and reasoning for each main task/subtask (e.g., paste key metrics, screenshots/figures if supported).
Update task statuses as you progress; mark completed items and note any blockers or failures with recovery steps.
Generate periodic progress summaries and analytics to monitor execution health and ensure all mandatory analyses are completed.
Check plan completion status before finalizing; handle any failures gracefully with retries or adjusted parameters.

tasks.description: Formatting and Final Output Production: Assemble and validate the final JSON deliverable exactly as specified by the user.

subtasks.description: Assemble the final JSON with two top-level keys: "portfolio" (array of {ticker, position, weight, reason}) and "suggestions" (array of {ticker, action, amount?, reason}). Ensure action values are one of: increase allocation | decrease allocation | drop position.
Validate all JSON fields: decimal amounts for weights and change amounts, omit "amount" for drop position, and ensure consistency with the documented changes and final portfolio_dict.
Output the Final Answer only after confirming all mandatory analyses were run on the initial and final portfolios and that at least 2–3 iterations showed progressive risk improvement.
